commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=47f6f1c6fd186b87788aebfaa9924dd14984f4d6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared `.docodex`/`.pptcodex`/`.xlscodex` suffix normalizer for agent file-tool `path` parameters and applies it to read, write/edit, workspace guard, and memory-flush paths with tests.

PR surface: Source +60, Tests +86. Total +146 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the execution-side gap: current main leaves a supplied `report.docodex` path unchanged through XML-only path normalization. I did not establish a deterministic live model turn that produces the hallucinated suffix.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide silent correction versus reject/revalidation semantics, and the external contributor must supply real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): auto-correct hallucinated docx/pptx/xlsx extensions in tool path params
target.body:
## Summary
Add auto-correction for model-hallucinated document-file extensions (`.docodex` → `.docx`, `.pptcodex` → `.pptx`, `.xlscodex` → `.xlsx`) at the shared agent-tool parameter boundary. The correction is applied to `path` parameters in file tools (read/write/edit) before filesystem execution, covering the same normalization pipeline that already strips malformed XML suffixes.

Fixes #93326

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Model-supplied `.docodex` paths (and similarly hallucinated PPTX/XLSX extensions) pass unchanged through the current path-normalization pipeline and cause file-operation failures.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13.1, Linux
- **Code analysis path**: `src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts` → `wrapToolParamValidation` / `normalizeDocumentExtensionFromKeys`, `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` → `createOpenClawReadTool` / host write/edit guard / memory-flush write guard
- **Test framework**: Vitest

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.params.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  49 passed (49)
   Start at  20:11:58
   Duration  6.25s
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Path parameters ending in `.docodex` are transparently corrected to `.docx` before they reach file tool execution. Existing XML-suffix normalization is preserved and the two corrections compose correctly (`.docodex</arg_value>>` → `.docx`).

**What was not tested**: Live model reproduction of the `.docodex` hallucination (the model-side trigger is intermittent and not deterministically reproducible). The execution-side gap — where a hallucinated path reaching the tool boundary passes through unchanged — is covered and verified by unit tests.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 49 passed across 5 test files (22 new tests for extension normalization) |
| Type Check | ✅ | `pnpm tsgo --project tsconfig.json` exit code 0 |
| Lint | ✅ | `oxlint --rules correctness` no issues |

## Risk checklist
**Did user-visible behavior change?** **No** — only hallucinated nonexistent extensions are auto-corrected; valid file paths with real extensions pass through unchanged.
**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** **No**
**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** **No** — the correction only rewrites the path parameter value; filesystem sandboxing and path guards remain in effect.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested
target.author: zhangqueping

commands.author: jontsai

target.title: fix(slack): allow channel-id reads for name-allowlisted channels
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Slack action tools can target a channel by Slack channel ID, for example when reading a permalink or operating on a message outside the current thread. If an operator configured Slack channel access by human-readable channel name (for example `#allowed-channel`) with `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` enabled, those channel-ID reads were rejected before the name allowlist could be applied. That blocked legitimate read-like Slack actions such as reading messages, downloading files, listing pins, and listing reactions for channels that were configured as allowed by name. ## How This Changes Behavior - Carries the current Slack channel name through threading tool context when the inbound transport provides it. - Uses the current channel name only when the target channel matches the current context, so one allowed context cannot authorize a different target channel. - Lazily resolves Slack conversation names through `conversations.info` when an action has only a channel ID and the account explicitly enabled name matching. - Re-checks read authorization with the resolved channel name and still fails closed if no allowed name is found. - Reuses the existing conversation info cache and returns names from that helper. ## Evidence - `pnpm test:extension slack` — 101 files / 1334 tests passed. - `pnpm vitest run extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/channel-type.test.ts` — 2 files / 74 tests passed. - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — passed. - `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` — passed. - Added synthetic-fixture coverage for: - current-context channel-name authorization of a matching channel ID; - rejecting a different target channel even when the current context has an allowed name; - resolving a channel name via `conversations.info` before failing an allowlist read; - resolving a name-keyed allow before applying wildcard fallback denial; - returning conversation names from the Slack channel-type helper. Not sure if this is acceptable proof, but in the past month, I had multiple OpenClaws on different machines collaborate with one another via a shared whitelisted Slack channel. Recently (as in the past ~3-5 days), after I upgraded to 2026.6.8, the ability for me to have bot-to-bot conversations went away. My use case was Bot A does dev work on a repo; Bot B does deployment of a said PR to servers C it has access to. A does not have access to C, but can do so indirectly through B. Example of it working from the past month: Example of it NOT working: Example of it working again after this patch: I'm having a bit of difficulty patching the source, reinstalling OpenClaw from source, and then generating this proof... As it's 6am now and I haven't slept, I'll have to try to resume this later. ## Privacy / Fixture Notes All examples in this PR use fabricated Slack fixtures such as `#allowed-channel`, `C0123456789`, and `C9876543210`; no private workspace channel names, channel IDs, user IDs, or message timestamps are included. ## Redacted Runtime Proof I installed and ran this branch from source on a local OpenClaw gateway, then exercised the Slack `message.read` path against a live Slack account. Real workspace/channel/user identifiers are intentionally replaced here with fabricated fixtures. ```text OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (ee83402) branch: fix/slack-read-allowlist-channel-name-public ``` Relevant redacted config shape: ```json5 { channels: { slack: { groupPolicy: "allowlist", dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: true, channels: { "#allowed-channel": { enabled: true } } } } } ``` Allowed-channel read by Slack channel ID: ```text action: message.read target: channel:C0123456789 threadId: 1234567890.123456 result: ok=true result.messages.length: 1 result.hasMore: true ``` Denied-channel read by Slack channel ID: ```text action: message.read target: channel:C9876543210 result.status: error result.error: Slack read target channel is not allowed. ``` This verifies the patched runtime accepts a Slack read where the action target is a Slack channel ID while the allowlist entry is a channel name resolved at runtime, and still fails closed for non-matching channel IDs.
target.author: jontsai
